data.title: Microservices architecture
data.description: YouTube uses a highly modular architecture with multiple services handling various tasks such as video playback, recommendation engine, and user authentication.
data.technical_spec: Services are typically containerized and deployed using Kubernetes.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A global CDN is used to ensure fast video delivery by caching content at edge servers close to users.
data.technical_spec: Integration with popular CDN providers, employing strategies like edge caching.

data.title: Data Storage Solutions
data.description: YouTube utilizes systems like Bigtable and Spanner for scalable storage solutions capable of handling vast amounts of video data.
data.technical_spec: Combines both SQL and NoSQL approaches for data throughput and consistency.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Sophisticated load balancing techniques ensure stability and responsiveness, distributing user requests efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes both hardware and software load balancers to balance incoming traffic across multiple servers.

data.title: Scalable Video Encoding
data.description: Efficient video encoding processes that allow for scalable uploads and adaptive streaming.
data.technical_spec: Use of codecs like VP9, AV1 to optimize bitrate and quality.
